rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (approve/lgtm), so both correctly find no real defects and neither fabricates anything grounded outside the diff. X's verification (upstream grep, no leftover references, matches ground-truth's clean approve) is tightly grounded and reaches the correct verdict; its non-blocking note is honest but not framed as an actionable ask. Y pads with low-value 'sweep' restatements of the diff and raises two comments (missing chat-completion test, add explanatory comment for naming) that are grounded but marginal/out-of-scope for a pure rename fix, landing on a COMMENT verdict that overstates the PR's issues relative to the actual lgtm outcome; its explicit file:line 'add X' asks do make it slightly more actionable in form.
